definition: I2S plants a high-value byte at a leading document offset to steer the document toward an attribute-rich parse state where the attribute count exceeds the namespace-slot count, satisfying a loop guard.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes CMP operands at the leading header byte offset (0x00), setting 0xf9 where losers have small values, steering the document toward an attribute-rich state where the loop condition i < no is satisfied.
signature.one_line: I2S sets leading header byte to produce attribute-count exceeding namespace count

analysis.summary_one_line: An element with enough attributes that the requested index exceeds the namespace-def count, forcing the property-list walk loop to iterate.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: XML whose parsed element carries enough attributes that the attribute index `no` (driven by the harness loop `for (i=0; i<n; i++)` over xmlTextReaderAttributeCount) exceeds the namespace-definition count, so after the ns loop at 2529 leaves `i < no` true and the property-list walk loop `for (;i < no;i++)` at 2540 must iterate. The leading 4-byte little-endian header at offset 0x00 (f9 00 00 00) sets parser options/sizes that produce this attribute-rich document state.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry f9 at offset 0x00 (vs the losers' small 06/10), and the winner-only lines 2541-2543 (cur = cur->next; if (cur == NULL) return(0);) are taken only on the W role, showing the property-list walk loop body executes. Hit-count on the branch is winner=5 vs loser=0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds have small leading-byte values (06, 10, 01) at offset 0x00, and on the L role line 2540 shows T=0 F=9 — the loop condition is never true, so the requested index never forces a property-list step; loser resolves only 2/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets, letting it set the leading header/comparand bytes that steer the document toward the attribute-rich state; naive must reach the same state by blind havoc byte mutation and largely fails (2/10 vs 9/10). I2S-floor dash rows in the winning seed's lineage confirm an I2S contribution.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the property-list walk loop that runs only when the requested attribute index still exceeds traversal after the namespace loop.
The attribute index `no` is supplied by the harness loop iterating over the element's attribute count.
The winner-only lines 2541-2543 (property-list walk body) are taken on the W role, evidencing the loop iterating.
Winner-resolving seeds carry f9 at offset 0x00 while loser-blocking seeds carry small values.
On the loser role the blocker loop condition is never true (T=0 F=9).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2540  T=6 F=6  T=0 F=9  for (;i < no;i++) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner hits the branch 5 times while the loser hits it 0 times, and the winner resolves 9/10 vs loser 2/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=5  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog enables the winner via I2SRandReplace operand substitution at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and reaches states only by havoc/token byte mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where forcing the attribute index `no` above the namespace-def count (so line 2540 must iterate) shows naive resolving the branch as often as cmplog, with no f9/header-byte dependence — meaning I2S operand substitution gives no advantage in reaching the property-list walk.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the offset-0x00 leading byte (f9 vs 06/10) to a large attribute count is inferred from the harness header convention, not directly traced through xmlReaderForMemory option parsing.
